caption: Individual-soldier collision, phase 3 (render-as-reality). The soldiers you see are now driven by the simulated bodies: each mark follows its `_sim_soldier_pos`, so the front-rank soldiers reflect the per-soldier, cross-regiment separation as two infantry lines clash head-on — they hold soldier-spacing across the contact instead of mushing through each other. All the flock polish (formation, combat lunge, rank-cycling, relief corridor, colour) is preserved on top. It stays non-authoritative — combat and movement still use the regiment circle, so outcomes are unchanged; only the rendered positions reflect the collision. (The effect is subtle at this stage; per-soldier combat in phase 4 is where it changes how battles play.)
